Using selectFrom(usersTable) fetches all columns from the users table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and impact performance. Replace with an explicit select specifying only the required columns, e.g., create.select(idField, nameField, ageField, statusField).from(usersTable).

Using `selectFrom(userTable)` fetches all columns from the table, but only id, name, and email are used. Replace with an explicit column selection like `dsl.select(idField, nameField, emailField).from(userTable)` to fetch only the required columns.

Using `selectFrom(userTable)` fetches all columns from the table when only id, name, and email are needed. Replace with `dsl.select(idField, nameField, emailField).from(userTable)` to explicitly select only the required columns.
